heading: Approval and examination
label: 80504 Approval and examination
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)80504.46 App.:1502(a) (related to approval and examination).(related to approval and examination), , .Pub. L. 95–208, § 3(a)91 Stat. 1476Dec. 13, 1977In this section, the words “Beginning on the date the instrument of ratification is deposited by the United States in accordance with the provisions of article VII of the Convention, for new containers, and beginning on , for existing containers” are omitted as obsolete.September 6, 1982In subsections (a)(1) and (b)(1), the words “government of another country that is a party to the Convention” are substituted for “administration of another contracting party to the Convention” for clarity.In subsection (c), the word “initial” is added for consistency in the section.

L. 95–208, § 3(a)91 Stat. 1476Dec. 13, 1977In this section, the words “Beginning on the date the instrument of ratification is deposited by the United States in accordance with the provisions of article VII of the Convention, for new containers, and beginning on , for existing containers” are omitted as obsolete.September 6, 1982In subsections (a)(1) and (b)(1), the words “government of another country that is a party to the Convention” are substituted for “administration of another contracting party to the Convention” for clarity.In subsection (c), the word “initial” is added for consistency in the section.

children.heading: .—Domicile and Principal Office in United States
children.label: a .—Domicile and Principal Office in United States

children.text: approved initially under procedures prescribed by the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating or by the government of another country that is a party to the Convention; and
examined periodically as provided in the Convention under procedures prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Domicile or Principal Office in United States
children.label: b .—Domicile or Principal Office in United States

children.text: approved initially under procedures prescribed by the Secretary or by the government of another country that is a party to the Convention; and

examined periodically as provided in the Convention, under procedures prescribed by the government of the country in which the owner is domiciled or has its principal office, as long as that country is a party to the Convention.

children.heading: .—Neither Domicile Nor Principal Office in United States
children.label: c .—Neither Domicile Nor Principal Office in United States
children.text: A container owner neither domiciled nor having its principal office in the United States or another country that is a party to the Convention may submit a container for initial approval and periodic examination under procedures prescribed by the Secretary.
